name: Amala
intro: Native to the Yoruba tribe in the western part of Nigeria, Amala is prepared using yam flour (elubo). The yam is dried and ground into a fine flour that is oﬀ-white in colour. When cooked or added to hot water, it then turns a deep brown colour. It has an earthy flavour and is eaten alongside traditional stews such as Efo Riro.
method: Pour 2 cups of cold water into a pot and add the yam flour all at once. Stir continuously with a wooden spoon over medium heat until it starts to thicken to a dough. This should take 6-8 minutes.

Pour in the remaining 1/2 cup of hot water, do not stir, cover the pot and simmer for 5-6 minutes over low heat. Mix and vigorously knead the amala until you are left with a smooth, soft, sticky dough.
Place the garri in a large heat resistant bowl and add 2 cups of boiling hot water. Mix thoroughly with a wooden spoon until it comes together and you have a sticky dough.
